claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know their age, and if you're claiming for multiple children, please tell me each of their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for that information! Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?

*(For an 8-year-old, this would typically be "yes" - most children this age aren't in paid employment.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 8!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

*(Again, for an 8-year-old, this would typically be "yes" - children this age don't usually receive these benefits in their own name.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

*(For an 8-year-old, the answer would be no - apprenticeships are for older young people.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's way too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! Let me move on to some questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and your family
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about your responsibility for Alex:**

Are you responsible for Alex? This means meeting **at least one** of the following:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount) - this includes paying for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute towards his upkeep - I help with things like clothes and school dinners.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Since you contribute towards Alex's upkeep (clothes, school dinners, etc.), you meet the responsibility requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question:**

Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example, does the person Alex lives with (perhaps another parent or guardian) already receive Child Benefit for him?

- **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex
- **Someone else is claiming**, but they have agreed to transfer the claim to you
- **Someone else is claiming**, and there's a dispute about who should receive it

Which situation applies?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex's living situation:**

Where does Alex normally live? Is Alex:

- **In hospital** (for an extended stay)
- **In local authority care**
- **In a foster care arrangement**
- **Living normally** at home (with another parent, guardian, family member, etc.)

Which best describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally - he lives with his other parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **NOT** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, Alex's living costs are being covered privately (by you, his other parent, or family) rather than by the local council?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, the council isn't paying anything - it's just us as parents looking after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! Based on your answers, I've completed the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on our assessment, you meet all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary:
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ Alex is not working, receiving benefits, or in an apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You contribute to Alex's upkeep (clothes, school dinners, etc.)
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✅ The local council is not paying for Alex's care

### Next Steps to Claim:
1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - if you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may have to pay some or all of it back through tax
5. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

📎 For more information and to apply: [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so he automatically meets the age requirement. He is not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and contributes to Alex's upkeep (clothes, school dinners), which satisfies the responsibility requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, he lives normally with his other parent, and the local council is not contributing to his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their child Alex. Although Alex doesn't live with the claimant, the claimant contributes to his upkeep, which satisfies the responsibility requirement. All other eligibility criteria are met, including UK residency and no conflicting claims.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so he automatically meets the age requirement. He is not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and contributes to Alex's upkeep (clothes, school dinners), which satisfies the responsibility requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, he lives normally with his other parent, and the local council is not contributing to his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their child Alex. Although Alex doesn't live with the claimant, the claimant contributes to his upkeep, which satisfies the responsibility requirement. All other eligibility criteria are met, including UK residency and no conflicting claims.
